response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array but for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

the first module defines the task of calculating the sum of absolute differences for each element in the array and specifies that for elements at positions that are multiples of 3 the sum should be of squared differences  

the second module outlines the constraints for the calculation, including the requirement that the array is sorted and that the result array must have the same length as the input array  

the third module specifies the conditions for the calculation, stating that for elements at positions that are multiples of 3 the squared difference is used instead of the absolute difference  

the fourth module ensures that the final result array is generated correctly by applying the specified calculation for each element in the array and returning the computed values  

***  

the first module calculates the sum of absolute differences for each element in the array and applies the squared difference calculation for elements at positions that are multiples of 3  

the second module ensures that the calculation is performed correctly for each element in the array, including the use of the squared difference for the specified positions  

the third module ensures that the calculation is applied correctly for each element in the array and that the result array is built with the correct values  

the fourth module ensures that the final result array is generated with the correct values and that all constraints are met for the calculation of the sum of absolute differences or squared differences for each element in the array
